data.description:
## Context

rebar hard-codes the git remote name `origin` at every ticket-branch network site,
so the shared ticket store MUST be reachable as `origin`. In our split-residency
setup (code reviewed via a `gerrit` remote; the `tickets` branch's source of truth
on GitHub for the GitHub Actions -> Jira sync) this forces `origin`=GitHub and
leaves no config seam. Parallels the earlier `tickets_branch()` migration (closed
task `rich-gravel-twain`) which centralized the *branch* name; this does the same
for the *remote* name.

## What

Add a `sync.remote` config key (default `"origin"`), resolved through the normal
precedence (config-file `[sync] remote` + `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` env, the same generic
mechanism as `sync.push`), exposed via a `tickets_remote()` helper mirroring
`tickets_branch()`, and thread it through every currently-hardcoded site.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `SyncConfig` gains `remote: str = "origin"` and `_SECTIONS["sync"]` coerces it
      in `src/rebar/config.py`; `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` works via the existing generic
      env layer (no bespoke env wiring) and participates in the config-cache signature.
- [ ] New `config.tickets_remote(root)` helper mirrors `config.tickets_branch(root)`.
- [ ] Every hard-coded `"origin"` network site uses the resolved remote:
      `src/rebar/_store/push.py` (presence guard + push/fetch/ref),
      `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` (`reconverge` fetch/verify + `_do_reconverge`),
      `src/rebar/_commands/fsck.py` (`_push_pending`),
      `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` (`_mount_or_create_branch` and
      `pending_init_attaches_to_existing`).
- [ ] Default behavior is unchanged when unset (still `origin`); the existing suite passes.
- [ ] New unit tests cover: default resolves to `origin`; `[sync] remote` config and
      `REBAR_SYNC_REMOTE` env each override; a network site uses the configured remote.
- [ ] Project docs updated for our actual setup (origin=GitHub tickets+mirror,
      gerrit=Gerrit code review): `CLAUDE.md` git-workflow section + the new key in
      `docs/config.md`.

## Out of scope

- Tier-2 init "split-residency awareness" (warn when the sync remote looks like a
  Gerrit host) — tracked separately.
- Changing this repo's actual remote layout (`origin` stays GitHub).
data.title: Make the tickets sync remote configurable (sync.remote, default origin)
